title: QUIC RFC 9000 Stateless Reset Versioning Architecture
purpose: Describe how the endpoint-owned version-profile snapshot keeps RFC 9000 Stateless Reset generation acceptable after connection-state loss.
scope:
This design covers the narrow RFC 9000 stateless-reset versioning slice. The runtime endpoint retains an immutable version-profile snapshot alongside the token and path state so a later Stateless Reset emission can still be framed from the connection's supported-version history after the connection runtime is disposed.
It does not widen supported versions, add new packet formats, or claim version-specific on-wire fields beyond the existing short-header Stateless Reset layout.
